summary: A symmetrical fortress-style structure featuring a central domed/rounded brick roof with a cross-shaped interior layout. The building is anchored by four tall corner towers with overhanging roofs, surrounded by cream-colored crenellated walls. The design emphasizes defensive architecture with multiple levels and strategic window placement.

shape.overall_form: Cross-shaped central structure with four corner towers

rebuild_hints: Start with four corner towers as vertical anchors, each approximately 2-3 blocks wide and 16+ blocks tall
Build cross-shaped central structure with walls connecting towers
Create domed roof using brick blocks in a rounded/hemispherical pattern over central area
Add crenellations using fence blocks and stone slabs around perimeter walls
Place windows in regular rows on tower faces and central walls
Add overhanging roof sections on top of each tower using stairs/slabs
Include cream/light-colored stone trim and crenellations for contrast
Ensure symmetry along both N-S and E-W axes
Interior cross-pattern visible in top-down view should guide wall placement
uncertainties: Exact block count for dome curvature - appears to use stacked brick but precise pattern unclear
Interior room layout and floor divisions not fully visible
Exact height of towers relative to central structure (appears 16-18 blocks total)
Whether windows are glass panes or dark openings - appears to be dark openings
Specific wood type used in interior framing
Exact dimensions of central cross arms
